title: Mortara ELI Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Lead Fail or Poor Signal Quality
description: Identifies external causes of lead failure or poor signal quality, including electrode placement, cable integrity, connector issues, and patient skin preparation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is actively undergoing an EKG procedure. Expected outcome: Safe environment for troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect Lead Cables and Connectors
steps.instructions: Check all lead wires for visible damage, fraying, or bent pins. Ensure connectors are fully seated in the device. Expected outcome: Damaged or loose cables are identified.

steps.title: Verify Electrode Quality and Placement
steps.instructions: Check that electrodes are within expiration, adhesive intact, and correctly placed. Ensure skin is clean, dry, and free from oils or lotions. Expected outcome: Proper electrode adhesion and placement improve signal quality.

steps.title: Swap Leads or Electrodes
steps.instructions: Replace suspected faulty leads with known good ones. Expected outcome: Signal improves or fault follows the lead, confirming a defective cable.

steps.title: Check Device Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm the correct lead configuration (e.g., 12-lead) is selected. Ensure gain and filter settings are appropriate for patient condition. Expected outcome: Device settings support optimal signal acquisition.

steps.title: Observe for External Interference
steps.instructions: Look for nearby electrical devices, cell phones, or monitors causing artifact. Expected outcome: Removing interference restores clean signals.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported intermittent or poor-quality EKG signals.
CCR.Cause: One lead cable was damaged and one electrode batch was expired.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced faulty lead cable and expired electrodes; verified signal quality restored.

helpfulDetails: Observed artifact type (e.g., 60 Hz noise, baseline wander)
Final device status: Functional after cable/electrode replacement

title: Mortara ELI Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Thermal Printer Failure
description: Identifies external causes of thermal printer failure, including paper jams, incorrect paper type, cable connections, and power issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is actively undergoing an EKG. Expected outcome: Safe environment for troubleshooting without affecting patient care.

steps.title: Check Printer Power
steps.instructions: Verify the printer is powered on and indicator lights are normal. Expected outcome: Printer powers on without error lights; ensures printer receives electricity.

steps.title: Inspect Paper Supply
steps.instructions: Ensure proper thermal paper is installed and seated correctly. Check for jams or misaligned paper. Expected outcome: Paper feeds smoothly; resolves most feed issues.

steps.title: Verify Printer Cables and Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect the printer’s data and power cables for secure connection and damage. Re-seat connections if necessary. Expected outcome: Printer communicates properly with EKG; eliminates connection faults.

steps.title: Test Printer with a Self-Test or Demo Print
steps.instructions: Use device menu to run a printer self-test (if available). Expected outcome: Printer prints test page; confirms printer hardware functionality.

steps.title: Check Device Settings
steps.instructions: Verify printer selection and paper type in EKG settings. Expected outcome: Correct configuration ensures print commands are sent properly.

steps.title: Power Cycle the EKG and Printer
steps.instructions: Turn both units off, wait 30 seconds, and power back on. Expected outcome: Resolves temporary software or communication glitches.

CCR.Complaint: Printer fails to print EKG strips.
CCR.Cause: Paper jam and misaligned feed roller identified during inspection.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared jam, reseated paper, verified successful self-test print.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (ready for patient use or OOS)

title: Mortara ELI Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Network or EMR Connectivity Failure
description: Identifies external causes of network or EMR connectivity failure, including cables, switches, network settings, EMR interface configuration, and software updates.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions:
Confirm no patient is actively undergoing an EKG procedure.
Expected outcome: Safe environment to troubleshoot without affecting patient care.

steps.title: Verify Power and Device Status
steps.instructions:
Ensure the EKG machine is powered on, battery is charged (if applicable), and no warning lights indicate system errors.
Expected outcome: Device is operational and ready to connect.

steps.title: Check Physical Network Connections
steps.instructions:
Inspect the Ethernet cable or Wi-Fi connection. Swap cables or test with a known-good port if needed.
Expected outcome: Solid physical connection; link lights on Ethernet port should be active.

steps.title: Confirm Network Access
steps.instructions:
Ping the device from the hospital network or attempt to access the EMR server from another workstation.
Expected outcome: Network is reachable; if not, escalate to IT/network support.

steps.title: Verify EMR Configuration
steps.instructions:
Check the device’s network settings, EMR interface settings, and IP addresses. Confirm they match hospital network requirements.
Expected outcome: Correct configuration; misconfigurations may prevent data transfer.

steps.title: Restart EKG and Network Equipment
steps.instructions:
Power-cycle the EKG machine, connected switches, or routers. Temporary software or network glitches often resolve with a restart.
Expected outcome: Connectivity restored without further intervention.

steps.title: Test with EMR/Network Export
steps.instructions:
Perform a test EKG export to EMR or network location. Confirm successful data transfer.
Expected outcome: Data is sent and received correctly.

CCR.Complaint: EKG machine will not send data to EMR.
CCR.Cause: Ethernet cable was loose; IP configuration mismatch on device.
CCR.Resolution: Secured cable and updated device network settings; verified successful EMR data transfer.

title: Mortara ELI Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Touchscreen Calibration or Errors
description: Identifies external causes of touchscreen issues, including calibration errors, screen misalignment, dirt or debris, software settings, and input response problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is actively undergoing an EKG procedure. Expected outcome: Safe environment for troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect the Touchscreen Surface
steps.instructions: Wipe the screen with a lint-free cloth and approved cleaning solution. Expected outcome: Remove debris that can interfere with touch detection.

steps.title: Check for Protective Covers or Screen Shields
steps.instructions: Remove any screen protectors or films that may impede touch sensitivity. Expected outcome: Restored proper touch response.

steps.title: Restart the Device
steps.instructions: Power cycle the EKG machine to reset software and touchscreen drivers. Expected outcome: Temporary glitches or calibration errors may be cleared.

steps.title: Verify Device Settings
steps.instructions: Navigate to touchscreen settings (if accessible) and ensure calibration mode is correctly configured. Expected outcome: Device recognizes touch inputs accurately.

steps.title: Perform a Touchscreen Calibration Routine
steps.instructions: Follow manufacturer-recommended calibration procedure from the settings menu. Expected outcome: Touch input aligns with displayed interface.

steps.title: Test Touch Response Across the Entire Screen
steps.instructions: Use a calibration or test pattern to check responsiveness in all areas. Expected outcome: No dead zones, erratic behavior, or delayed response.

CCR.Complaint: Touchscreen unresponsive and misaligned reported by staff.
CCR.Cause: Dirt on screen and calibration drift identified during troubleshooting.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned screen and recalibrated touchscreen; device now responsive.

helpfulDetails: Check for screen cracks or damage
Note if protective film or overlay was removed

title: Mortara ELI Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Battery Not Charging
description: Identifies external causes for battery charging issues, including power source, battery connection, battery age, and charging system errors.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions:
Confirm no patient is undergoing an EKG procedure.
Expected outcome: Safe environment for troubleshooting without risk to patient monitoring.

steps.title: Check AC Power Connection
steps.instructions:
Verify the power cord is properly seated in both the wall outlet and the device.
Confirm the outlet is live using a known-working device or outlet tester.
Expected outcome: Device receives AC power, eliminating outlet issues.

steps.title: Inspect the Power Cord and Adapter
steps.instructions:
Check for visible damage, fraying, or loose connections.
Swap with a verified working cord/adapter if available.
Expected outcome: Confirm power delivery to device is stable.

steps.title: Verify Battery Seating and Contacts
steps.instructions:
Remove the battery and reseat it, ensuring proper alignment with contacts.
Inspect battery contacts for dirt, corrosion, or debris; clean gently if necessary.
Expected outcome: Proper electrical connection between battery and device.

steps.title: Check Battery Status Indicators
steps.instructions:
Observe any charging LEDs or on-screen battery icons.
Compare readings to device manual expectations (fully charging, low battery, error).
Expected outcome: Determine if the device recognizes the battery and attempts charging.

steps.title: Test with a Known-Good Battery (if available)
steps.instructions:
Insert a verified functional battery to see if charging occurs.
Expected outcome: Confirms whether the issue is with the battery or device charging circuit.

steps.title: Software or Firmware Check
steps.instructions:
Ensure device firmware is up to date; some devices suspend charging if firmware is outdated.
Expected outcome: Charging function not blocked by software errors.

CCR.Complaint: EKG battery not charging, device shows low battery warning.
CCR.Cause: Battery contacts misaligned and charging system not engaging.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated battery and tested power cord; device now charging normally.
